articles.headline: IndusInd Bank swings to ₹533 crore profit in March quarter
articles.summary: IndusInd Bank reported a standalone net profit of ₹533 crore for Q4 FY26, a significant recovery from the ₹2,236 crore loss in the year-ago period. The results were driven by healthy growth in net interest income and fee income. Deposits and advances saw a year-on-year decline of 3% and 8% respectively, while the bank focused on rebuilding trust and transitioning to a growth mindset for FY27.

articles.headline: Indian OMCs step up spot LPG purchases from the United States
articles.summary: State-run oil marketing companies are increasing spot purchases of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) from the US due to ongoing supply uncertainty in West Asia. India has expanded its sourcing from 10 to 15 countries. While West Asian inflows dropped to 34% of total imports in late March, OMCs have secured 800,000 tonnes of inbound cargoes from the US, Russia, and Australia to ensure supply.

articles.headline: IT firms face revenue lag despite record AI-led deal wins
articles.summary: Major Indian IT services firms like TCS and Infosys are seeing a gap between strong contract values and actual revenue growth. The shift toward large, AI-led, outcome-based contracts has extended the conversion time from 4-6 months to nearly a year. TCV reached $68.7 billion in FY26 against $60 billion in revenue, as complex integrations and staged rollouts delay financial recognition.

articles.headline: SkyHop Aviation cleared to launch India’s first scheduled seaplane services
articles.summary: SkyHop Aviation, led by Avani Singh, is set to receive its air operator permit after successfully clearing regulatory trials. This will make it India’s first dedicated scheduled seaplane provider. The company has secured a Twin Otter DHC-6-400 aircraft and built dedicated jetty infrastructure at five islands in Lakshadweep. Initial services will connect mainland India to the archipelago.

articles.headline: Indian markets slide for third session amid IT rout, high oil
articles.summary: Domestic equity markets fell for the third straight day, with the Nifty 50 shedding 1.14% to end at 23,898. The sell-off was triggered by a 10.3% plunge in the Nifty IT index following weak earnings outlooks from Infosys and HCL Tech. Rising Brent crude prices, now above $100 a barrel, and downgrades by JP Morgan and HSBC further dampened investor sentiment regarding rich valuations.

articles.headline: Yamaha Aerox E review: Electric scooter fails performance expectations
articles.summary: The electrification of Yamaha’s flagship Aerox scooter has disappointed reviewers. The Aerox E, featuring two removable 1.5-kWh batteries, lacks the punchy performance and comfort associated with the regular ICE version. With a range of 117 km and a rumored price exceeding ₹2 lakh, the scooter's firm suspension and compromised ergonomics make it a tough sell compared to the Aerox 155.

articles.headline: Reliance Jio Q4 net profit rises 13% to ₹7,935 crore
articles.summary: Reliance Jio Infocomm reported a 13% year-on-year increase in net profit for the March quarter, driven by organic ARPU growth and a better subscriber mix. Total users reached 524.4 million with ARPU rising to ₹214. Data traffic surged 35% to 66 billion GB. RIL Chairman Mukesh Ambani noted that the company is advancing steadily toward the listing of Jio Platforms.

articles.headline: JioStar Q4 profit plummets 52% despite revenue growth
articles.summary: Reliance Industries' media arm, JioStar, saw its sequential profit drop by 52% to ₹419 crore in Q4 FY26. Despite the profit dip, revenue increased by 22% to ₹9,784 crore. The network maintained a leading viewership share of 34.2% nationally. JioHotstar averaged 500 million monthly active users, setting a peak concurrency record of 72.5 million during the T20 Men's Cricket World Cup Final.

articles.headline: Reliance Retail reports steady Q4 growth with 20,000 store milestone
articles.summary: Reliance Retail Ventures posted gross revenue of ₹98,232 crore for the March quarter, up 10.8% year-on-year. Profit after tax rose marginally to ₹3,563 crore. The company expanded its network to 20,160 outlets. Digital and quick commerce channels saw significant traction, with JioMart daily orders tripling. The retail giant's FMCG brands, Campa and Independence, doubled revenue for the year.

articles.headline: Hindustan Zinc profit surges 68% on record silver prices
articles.summary: Vedanta Group’s Hindustan Zinc reported its highest profit growth in 20 quarters, with Q4 net profit rising 68% to ₹5,033 crore. The surge was attributed to sharp increases in silver and metal prices and lower domestic coal costs. Silver revenue more than doubled to ₹4,032 crore. The company plans a capital expenditure of $500-$600 million for FY27 to support its record metal production.

articles.headline: India mulls stake transfer at Chabahar port to Iranian entity
articles.summary: Ahead of the expiry of a US sanctions waiver, India is considering temporarily transferring its stake in the Chabahar port to an Iranian entity. New Delhi is in talks with both Washington and Tehran to protect its $120 million investment. Officials noted that India does not plan a complete exit, as it remains committed to expanding connectivity through a future rail link at the harbor.

articles.headline: CBIC sets three-day limit for processing export duty scrolls
articles.summary: To support exporters facing high freight costs, the Central Board of Indirect Taxes & Customs has ordered that RoDTEP and RoSCTL scrolls be processed within three days. These schemes rebate embedded taxes and levies on exported products. The government has also extended the RoDTEP scheme at fully restored rates for six months until September 30 to help firms navigate global disruptions.

articles.headline: Tesla releases three-row Model Y L in India at ₹61.99 lakh
articles.summary: Tesla has expanded its Indian portfolio with the release of the Model Y L, a long-wheelbase electric SUV priced at ₹61.99 lakh. The variant features a 2+2+2 seating layout, dual-motor all-wheel drive, and a range of up to 681 km. Targeted at premium families, the vehicle can accelerate from 0-100 kph in approximately 5 seconds.

articles.headline: SEBI proposes revamp of stockbroker variable net worth norms
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India has proposed new methodology for calculating the variable net worth of stockbrokers. The draft paper seeks to align capital requirements with operational risks and client base size. Proposed rules include maintaining additional capital of ₹50 lakh for brokers with over 10,000 clients, ensuring a financial cushion to absorb unforeseen losses.

articles.headline: IIT-Ropar launches India’s first fully integrated agri intelligence system
articles.summary: The Indian Institute of Technology, Ropar, has launched ANNAM.AI, the nation’s first fully integrated agricultural intelligence ecosystem. The system features advanced weather stations, AI-driven call centres, and a multilingual chat engine. It provides hyperlocal weather alerts and crop diagnostics to help farmers reduce water usage by 30% and prevent up to 12% of crop loss.

articles.headline: IGC cuts global grain output projection by 3 million tonnes
articles.summary: The International Grains Council (IGC) has lowered its global foodgrain production projection for 2026-27 to 2,414 million tonnes. The cut is attributed to supply chain disruptions and fertilizer affordability issues linked to the West Asia conflict. Despite the downward revision, production is still expected to reach a record 2,474 million tonnes in the 2025/26 cycle.

articles.headline: Amazon partners with Bayer’s TGRA for sustainable rice carbon credits
articles.summary: The Good Rice Alliance (TGRA) has signed a long-term agreement with Amazon for the sale of carbon credits generated from sustainable rice farming in India. The initiative involves 13,000 small farmers and aims to reduce methane emissions—a pollutant 27 times more potent than CO2. Amazon will purchase credits covering 685,000 metric tonnes of CO2 equivalent in the initial phase.

articles.headline: Reservoir levels in India drop to 40% of total capacity
articles.summary: The Central Water Commission (CWC) revealed that water storage in India’s 166 major reservoirs has dropped to 40% of their 183.565 BCM capacity. Two-thirds of reservoirs are currently below 50% capacity, with southern states like Telangana and Karnataka particularly stressed at 21% and 26% respectively. Predicted heatwaves are expected to further deplete levels in the coming weeks.

articles.headline: Agro-forestry to be renamed tree-based farming to boost marginal incomes
articles.summary: Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan announced that 'agro-forestry' will be renamed 'tree-based farming' to encourage smallholders to adopt integrated farming. Addressing a northern states conference, he emphasized moving beyond cereal-centric crops to include dairy and beekeeping. The government will implement zonal agriculture roadmaps to ensure climate resilience and better seed quality.

articles.headline: Government announces ₹5,000 crore incentive for state mining auctions
articles.summary: The Mines Ministry has introduced a ₹5,000 crore financial incentive to encourage states to expedite the auction and operationalisation of mining blocks. The first-come, first-serve scheme rewards states for streamlining processes and adopting technology-driven monitoring. The move is intended to enhance domestic mineral production and support India's self-reliance in critical sectors.

articles.headline: Peak power demand hits seasonal high of 240 GW in April
articles.summary: India's peak power demand reached 240.12 GW on Thursday, driven by intense heat waves and increased air conditioner usage. This marks the highest supply level so far this summer. Experts predict demand may soon exceed the September 2023 record of 243.27 GW. Last summer, the peak demand recorded in June was 242.77 GW, while the all-time record stands at 250 GW from May 2024.

articles.headline: L&T enters B2B industrial electronics space with Coimbatore plant
articles.summary: Infrastructure giant L&T has commenced industrial electronics manufacturing at its Coimbatore campus in Tamil Nadu. The new business vertical, L&T Electronic Products & Systems (LTEPS), will develop power electronics, industrial robotics, and automation systems. Future expansion is planned across 40 acres to cover the entire electronics value chain, supporting India's self-reliance in manufacturing.

articles.headline: IRFC approves ₹13,615 crore loan for Telangana’s Hyderabad Metro takeover
articles.summary: The Indian Railway Finance Corporation has agreed to provide a ₹13,615 crore loan to the Telangana government to facilitate the takeover of Phase I of the Hyderabad Metro Rail from L&T. The loan features a 20-year repayment period. The state cabinet had previously approved the acquisition of a 100% equity stake in the project, which will now transition to government ownership.

articles.headline: US to levy preliminary anti-dumping duties on Indian solar cells
articles.summary: The US Department of Commerce has announced preliminary anti-dumping duties on imports of solar cells and panels from India. The move follows allegations that Indian manufacturers, including Mundra Solar and Premier Energy, are selling products below fair market value. The action targets India's growing export footprint in the renewable energy technology sector.

articles.headline: Infosys approves ₹52 crore performance-based stock grant for CEO
articles.summary: The board of Infosys has approved annual stock incentives worth ₹51.75 crore for CEO Salil Parekh. The grants, in the form of restricted stock units (RSUs), include performance-linked and ESG-based targets. Vesting will occur over one to two years, subject to meeting cumulative relative shareholder return benchmarks and other internal milestones defined by the remuneration committee.

articles.headline: International passenger traffic at Indian airports drops 18.5% in March
articles.summary: Passenger footfalls for international travel fell sharply to 5.3 million in March as the conflict in West Asia disrupted flight schedules. Total passenger traffic at Indian airports declined 4.4% to 34.5 million. Airports in Kochi and Hyderabad saw 12% declines, while smaller hubs like Nagpur and Kannur reported drops exceeding 90%. Outbound travel demand remains muted due to high airfares.

articles.headline: Delhi Airport Pier C ready for international operations in May
articles.summary: Delhi International Airport Limited (DIAL) has completed the conversion of Pier C at Terminal 3 from domestic to international use. The facility is set to begin operations in the first week of May. The move will increase the airport’s international handling capacity by approximately 50%, adding space for nearly 10 million additional passengers annually to support hub ambitions.

articles.headline: Iran, US Envoys Set to Meet in Pakistan
articles.summary: US envoys, including Jared Kushner, are heading to Pakistan to meet Iran's foreign minister. The talks aim to revive ceasefire discussions amid global tensions over Iran's nuclear program and energy exports.

articles.headline: Forced Continuation of Pregnancy Violates Dignity, Says Top Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court permitted a 15-year-old girl to terminate her over 28-week pregnancy, stating forced continuation violates dignity and autonomy, prioritizing the woman's welfare over procedural limitations.

articles.headline: Modi, Mamata Ramp Up Attacks as Bengal Battle Hits Home Stretch
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi and WB CM Mamata Banerjee traded barbs over Jadavpur University, escalating attacks ahead of the second phase of West Bengal polls. Modi accused TMC of anarchy; Banerjee called his remarks insulting.

articles.headline: Betting on Everything — Including State Secrets
articles.summary: Prediction markets like Polymarket and Kalshi are attracting bets on various events, from elections to state secrets. Cases reveal potential for insider trading and market manipulation.

articles.headline: Spl Rooms Set Up at Hospitals for Heatstrokes
articles.summary: State health department has opened dedicated treatment facilities for heat-related illnesses across government hospitals, equipping them with cooling facilities to stabilize patients.

articles.headline: 2 Men Dead After Being Struck by Lightning in Bidar
articles.summary: Two farmers were killed by lightning in Bidar district, Karnataka, while taking shelter under a tree during a thunderstorm. The incident occurred while they were working in their fields.

articles.headline: Man Held at Airport with ₹87.5L Drugs: Officials
articles.summary: A passenger arriving from Bangkok was arrested at Bengaluru Airport with hydroponic ganja valued at ₹87.5 lakh. The accused was booked under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) Act.

articles.headline: HC Stays Rape Trial Against Son of Expelled BJP Leader, Orders Child Support
articles.summary: Karnataka HC stayed criminal proceedings against an expelled BJP leader's son accused of rape, ordering him to pay ₹75,000 monthly for child support, noting consensual relationship and lack of means.

articles.headline: 6 People Held for Killing Realtor in Vijayapura
articles.summary: Six people were arrested for the murder of a realtor in Vijayapura, Karnataka, allegedly over a property dispute. The accused rammed his jeep, assaulted him, and shot him, causing fatal injuries.

articles.headline: Man Kills Wife Over Suspicion of Bringing an Affair in Bagalkote
articles.summary: A man killed his wife in Bagalkote, Karnataka, on suspicion of an extra-marital affair. The incident occurred at the neighbour's house where the deceased was present.

articles.headline: Leaders Across Party Lines Hail Record Turnout in TN
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu recorded a record 85.15% turnout in its assembly elections. Leaders across parties, including CM MK Stalin and actor Vijay, congratulated citizens for their "commendable" civic responsibility.

articles.headline: 2 Cops Died Due to Health Issues During Election Duty, Say Police
articles.summary: Two Tamil Nadu police personnel, a DSP and an SSI, died due to sudden health complications while on election duty during assembly elections. Officials cited health issues as the cause.

articles.headline: Coast Guard Chopper Seen Flying Low Over AP Council Chairman Notice
articles.summary: The AP Legislative Council Chairman rejected the resignation of MLC Jayamangala Venkataramana after the HC directed him to complete the inquiry within four weeks, finding his reasons unsatisfactory.

articles.headline: NHRC Issued Notice to TN Govt Over Fireworks Unit Blast
articles.summary: NHRC issued notices to Tamil Nadu govt and Virudhunagar SP after a firecracker unit blast killed 23 and injured others. The blast reportedly occurred due to friction during chemical mixing.

articles.headline: Cabinet Rejig, Leadership Back in Focus
articles.summary: Senior Karnataka Congress leaders met to review government decision-making and coordination, with discussions on leadership change and cabinet reshuffle after assembly election results.

articles.headline: 10 Injured After Tanker, Bus and Auto Collide in Kalaburagi
articles.summary: One person died and nine sustained injuries in a chain collision involving a cement tanker, bus, and autorickshaw in Kalaburagi, Karnataka. The tanker allegedly collided head-on with a bus.

articles.headline: AAP Crisis: MPs Revolt, Deserting Kejriwal Amid Punjab Poll Prep
articles.summary: Seven out of 10 AAP Rajya Sabha MPs are quitting the party, with three joining the BJP, causing political turmoil ahead of Punjab polls. Insiders suggest the party miscalculated the threat from the BJP.

articles.headline: NDA’s Rajya Sabha Strength Set to Get a Boost After New Entries
articles.summary: The BJP-led NDA's strength in the Rajya Sabha is set to increase as seven AAP MPs announced their merger with the BJP, boosting the NDA's tally and potentially impacting the government's legislative agenda.

articles.headline: 20 Jailed for Life in ’18 Assam Lynching Case
articles.summary: A special court in Assam sentenced 20 accused to life imprisonment in connection with a 2018 mob lynching case that killed two engineers. The court refused the prosecution's plea for the death penalty.

articles.headline: El Nino Set to Take Hold in May-July
articles.summary: An El Niño event is expected by mid-2026, potentially strong, signalling a difficult season for India. This coincides with record low snow reserves in the Himalayas.

articles.headline: 10 of 152 ACs in First Phase Saw an Absolute Fall in Voter Turnout
articles.summary: Ten assembly constituencies in West Bengal likely saw a decline in voter turnout in absolute terms compared to 2024 Lok Sabha polls. This occurred despite a growing electorate in the state.

articles.headline: HC Strikes Down EC Curbs on Motorcycle Riding in Bengal
articles.summary: Calcutta High Court ruled Election Commission cannot impose blanket restrictions on motorcycle riding during West Bengal polls, finding no provision in law to justify the curb.

articles.headline: TN Registered 85.15% Voting in April 23 Polls: EC
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu recorded its highest-ever assembly election turnout of 85.15% on April 23. Karur and Salem districts topped the charts with over 90% voting.

articles.headline: Poll Body Orders Suspension of OC in Hingalganj
articles.summary: Election Commission ordered suspension of Hingalganj police station OC for alleged partisan behaviour during West Bengal polls. The OC was accused of bias and nexus with local elements.

articles.headline: ED Investigates Foreign Card Use in Naxal Areas
articles.summary: ED is probing foreign debit card usage in Naxal-hit regions, finding suspicious withdrawals totaling ₹6.5 crore. The cards were linked to a global Christian mission not registered under FCRA.

articles.headline: RSS Isn’t Indian Version of KKK, Says RSS General Secy
articles.summary: RSS General Secretary Dattatreya Hosabale clarified the organization's goals in Washington, stating the perception of RSS as Hindu supremacist and anti-minority is a misunderstanding.

articles.headline: Centre Tells Zee5 to Not Release Bishnoi Series
articles.summary: Centre advised OTT platform ZEE5 to withhold its docuseries on Lawrence Bishnoi, citing concerns that it glorifies gangsterism and could impact public order, based on Punjab Police inputs.

articles.headline: 4 Killed in Churu Collision
articles.summary: Four people died after an SUV collided with a dumper truck in Churu, Rajasthan. Three were charred at the site, while the fourth succumbed later. Four others are undergoing treatment.

articles.headline: SC Refers Michel’s Plea for Release to Another Bench
articles.summary: Supreme Court referred Christian Michel's plea for release in the AgustaWestland case to another bench, as he claims to have completed the maximum sentence period after extradition.

articles.headline: TN Woodcutter Gets Death for Raping 5 Minors
articles.summary: A special court in Sivaganga awarded the death penalty to a 45-year-old woodcutter for sexually assaulting five minor girls, calling it a 'rarest of rare crime'. He was also fined ₹11,000.

articles.headline: 24 FIRs Filed Against Revenue Officials, Land Grabbers in J&K
articles.summary: J&K ACB registered 24 FIRs against revenue officials and land grabbers for fraudulent usurpation of prime custodian land parcels, unearthing major irregularities in land records.

articles.headline: Niti Aayog V-C, Scientist to Become Member
articles.summary: Outgoing BJP MLA Ashok Lahiri and scientist Gobardhan Das will become Niti Aayog vice-chairman and member, respectively, bringing their expertise to the government think tank.

articles.headline: US Lawmakers Slam Trump for Sharing ‘Hellhole’ Post
articles.summary: US lawmakers condemned Trump's sharing of a conservative talk show transcript calling India a 'hellhole', calling the rhetoric offensive and undermining US values and its partnership with India.

articles.headline: Putin May Attend G20 Meet in US: Kremlin
articles.summary: Kremlin stated Russian President Vladimir Putin may attend the G20 summit in Miami. The US had invited Russia, suggesting it was a mistake to expel them from G8 previously.

articles.headline: China Plans to Send Pandas to US Ahead of Trump-Xi Meeting
articles.summary: China is sending two pandas to the US, signaling stabilising ties ahead of President Trump's meeting with Xi Jinping. This is part of China's 'Panda Diplomacy' strategy.

articles.headline: US Fed Chair Powell
articles.summary: The US Justice Department dropped a criminal probe into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, clearing the way for his replacement's Senate confirmation amid concerns over President Trump's attacks.

articles.headline: Putin Renames FSB’s Spy Academy
articles.summary: President Vladimir Putin ordered Russia's FSB spy academy to be named after 'Iron Felix' Dzerzhinsky, founder of the Soviet secret police, honouring his contribution to state security.

articles.headline: India, US Agree to Remain Engaged After Trade Talks
articles.summary: India and the US agreed to remain engaged after three days of trade talks in Washington, signaling progress towards a balanced, mutually beneficial agreement and a target of $500 billion bilateral trade by 2030.

articles.headline: Paris Airports’ Operator Cuts Stake in GMR in $1 Billion Deal
articles.summary: Paris Airports’ operator ADP agreed to sell up to 7.3% of GMR Airports Ltd for $1.08 billion to a vehicle connected to GMR's founding family, aiming to crystallize investment value.

articles.headline: Morbi Ceramic Units Eye Revival as Gujarat Gas Ups PNG Supply
articles.summary: Morbi's ceramic industry is set to return to full capacity as Gujarat Gas increases piped natural gas supply. Many units had shut down due to high gas prices and limited supply.

articles.headline: SAIL Wins Court Block on Steel Antitrust Investigation
articles.summary: An Indian court put an antitrust investigation into state-run SAIL on hold after the company challenged the CCI for procedural lapses, claiming it was exonerated in a prior investigation.

articles.headline: Critical Defect Drives JLR to Recall Over 170,000 Vehicles
articles.summary: Jaguar Land Rover is recalling over 170,000 vehicles globally due to a critical drive power defect, potentially costing over $130 million and impacting customer confidence.

articles.headline: Kohli, Padikkal Lead RCB’s Demolition Act Against GT
articles.summary: Virat Kohli scored 81 and Devdutt Padikkal hit 55, guiding RCB to a five-wicket win over Gujarat Titans. Dropped on zero, Kohli anchored the chase after Padikkal's aggressive start.

articles.headline: Captain Hardik Faces the Heat for MI’s Poor Show
articles.summary: Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya faces intense scrutiny as the team languishes at eighth in the IPL table, having lost five of their first seven matches. His form and tactical choices are questioned.

articles.headline: India Submits EOI to Host 2038 Asian Games
articles.summary: India has officially submitted its expression of interest to host the 2038 Asian Games, marking a significant step in its strategy to become a global hub for major multi-sporting events.

articles.headline: ‘Loss Against Sindarov was the Turning Point’
articles.summary: R Praggnanandhaa believes his loss to Javokhir Sindarov in Round 3 of the Candidates tournament was a turning point, impacting his momentum and confidence, though his sister's win offers some solace.

articles.headline: Thomas Cup: Ayush, Sat-Chi Guide India to 5-8 4-1 Win Against Canada
articles.summary: Ayush Shetty and the doubles pair Satwiksairaj Rankireddy/Chirag Shetty secured wins as India beat Canada 4-1 in their Thomas Cup opener, placing India second in Group A behind China.

articles.headline: Uber Cup: India Lose to Denmark
articles.summary: India lost 2-3 to hosts Denmark in their opening Uber Cup tie. PV Sindhu won her match, but Unnati Hooda and the doubles pair lost crucial rubbers, leading to the overall defeat.

articles.headline: Shambhavi And Divyanshu Win Mixed Air Rifle Gold, Set World Record Score
articles.summary: Shambhavi Kshirsagar and Divyanshu Dewangan won the 10m air rifle mixed team gold with a world record score of 499.9 at the ISSF Junior World Cup, leading India's medal tally.

articles.headline: Bhullar Stands Tall as Fault Lines Appear
articles.summary: Gaganjeet Bhullar was the sole Indian to make the cut at the Singapore Open, highlighting a recurring pattern of Indian golfers failing to perform internationally despite perceived talent.

articles.headline: Group ADP to Sell 7.3% Stake in GMR Airports for ₹10,100 Crore
articles.summary: French group ADP will reduce its stake in GMR Airports from 32.3% to 25% by selling a 7.3% share to the GMR Promoter Group for over ₹10,100 crore. GMR Airports operates major hubs, including Delhi and Hyderabad. The deal involves a multi-layered transaction to restructure holdings while maintaining the partnership between the two entities.

articles.headline: Tech Layoffs Intensify: 92 Companies Cut 92,272 Jobs in Four Months
articles.summary: Mass layoffs in the engineering and tech sectors continue as 92 companies cut 92,272 jobs in early 2026. Meta announced plans to slash 10% of its workforce (8,000 employees), while Microsoft is targeting a 7% reduction through voluntary exits. The trend is largely driven by companies pivoting towards Artificial Intelligence and restructuring for cost efficiency.

articles.headline: Sensex Crashes 1,000 Points as IT Stocks Face Heavy Sell-off
articles.summary: The BSE Sensex plummeted 1,000 points (1.3%) to 76,664, led by a 5.3% crash in the Nifty IT index. The slump followed weak revenue guidance from Infosys and HCL Tech and rising crude prices due to Middle East tensions. Infosys shares fell 7.1%, marking a significant weekly loss for the sector as investors worried about discretionary spending and AI shifts.

articles.headline: IDBI Bank Disinvestment Process to Continue: Nirmala Sitharaman
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman confirmed that the IDBI Bank disinvestment plan is active, refuting rumors of its cancellation. The government aims to sell its 30.48% stake, while LIC plans to exit its 30.24% holding. Following the announcement, IDBI Bank shares surged 8%. The minister also discussed implementing an 'Open Architecture' model for bank insurance sales.

articles.headline: IndusInd Bank Reports Q4 Net Profit of ₹594 Crore
articles.summary: IndusInd Bank posted a consolidated net profit of ₹594 crore for Q4, a sharp recovery from the ₹2,329 crore loss in the same period last year. The profit was supported by lower provisions and the reversal of previous accounting errors in microfinance and derivative segments. Net Interest Income (NII) grew 43.4% year-on-year to ₹4,371.5 crore.

articles.headline: Stellantis India Reaches 50,000 Production Milestone in Tiruvallur
articles.summary: Stellantis India announced the production of its 50,000th vehicle from its Tiruvallur manufacturing facility. The unit focuses on the 'Smart Car' platform developed for India and international markets. The achievement highlights the company's growth in the domestic market and the success of local R&D and industrialization efforts led by its Indian teams.

articles.headline: Hindustan Zinc Posts Record Quarterly Profit of ₹5,033 Crore
articles.summary: Hindustan Zinc achieved a record consolidated net profit of ₹5,033 crore in Q4, representing a 68% year-on-year increase. The performance was bolstered by higher silver prices, record production volumes, and cost reduction measures. Revenue from operations climbed 49% to reach a peak of ₹13,544 crore during the final quarter of the fiscal year.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds CCI Power to Close Informant Cases
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the Competition Commission of India (CCI) is not obligated to hear or notify informants before closing a case at the preliminary stage under Section 26(2). The court dismissed a petition by Karnataka Power Corporation against a decision involving Singareni Collieries, affirming the regulator's administrative discretion in identifying prima facie violations.

articles.headline: JSW Steel and JFE Steel Form ₹32,000 Crore Joint Venture
articles.summary: JSW Steel and Japan’s JFE Steel have announced a 50:50 joint venture to set up a specialized steel plant in Odisha with an investment of ₹32,000 crore. The JV, named 'JSW JFE Electrical Steel Limited,' aims to produce high-grade steel for the engineering and construction sectors. The Sambalpur plant's capacity is expected to reach 10 million tonnes per annum by 2030.

articles.headline: Infosys Shares Hit 52-Week Low on Weak Revenue Outlook
articles.summary: Infosys shares dropped 7.09% to end at ₹1,154.45 after the company projected a conservative revenue growth of 1.5% to 3.5% for the upcoming fiscal year. Concerns over reduced discretionary spending by global clients and the impact of AI on traditional IT services dampened investor sentiment. Brokerages like Motilal Oswal and Nomura lowered their target prices for the stock.

articles.headline: Government Bond Yields Soften Amid High Volatility; Rupee Falls
articles.summary: Indian government bond yields ended slightly lower at 6.94% after a volatile trading session influenced by crude price spikes and US Treasury movements. Meanwhile, the Indian rupee fell 0.16% to 94.26 against the US dollar. Analysts attribute the currency's weakness to central bank easing of currency rules and persistent global geopolitical tensions affecting oil costs.

articles.headline: SEBI Allows Net Settlement of FPI Trades to Lower Costs
articles.summary: SEBI has implemented a new framework allowing Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) to settle trades on a net basis rather than gross. The move, effective until December 31, 2026, aims to reduce liquidity requirements and transaction costs for foreign funds. Net settlement will apply to offsetting buy and sell orders within a settlement cycle, helping manage funding pressures.

articles.headline: SEBI Proposes New Net Worth Calculation Rules for Stock Brokers
articles.summary: SEBI has issued a consultation paper proposing a new method to calculate the net worth of stock brokers. The proposal links net worth requirements to 10% of the average daily client cash balance or the number of active clients. The change follows the 'upstream' mechanism where client funds are moved to clearing corporations, reducing the cash held directly by brokers. Feedback is due by May 15.

articles.headline: India and US Discuss Interim Trade Agreement in Washington
articles.summary: Indian and US officials held four days of 'meaningful and constructive' talks in Washington DC to finalize an interim trade agreement. The discussions focused on market access, non-tariff measures, and digital trade. Both nations are working towards a Comprehensive Bilateral Trade Agreement (BTTA) to reach a $500 billion trade target, addressing long-standing concerns on both sides.

articles.headline: Regulation Should Promote Economic Activity, Not Just Compliance: IRDAI Chairman
articles.summary: IRDAI Chairman Ajay Seth emphasized that India's financial regulations must evolve into 'smart regulation' to support economic growth. Speaking at a Global Law School event, he urged banks to bolster IT systems and data security to protect consumers. He stated that the goal of 2047 requires a balance between risk assessment and encouraging domestic investment despite global headwinds.

articles.headline: Centre Allocates ₹5,000 Crore Incentive for Mining Sector Reforms
articles.summary: The Union Government has launched a ₹5,000 crore incentive scheme to accelerate mine operations and mineral production through 2026-27. States and UTs can receive up to ₹550 crore for implementing five key mining reforms by December 15, 2026. The initiative aims to boost state revenues, improve administrative efficiency, and increase mineral output across the country.

articles.headline: MNRE Launches Pilot CFD Scheme for 500 MW Renewable Energy
articles.summary: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) has introduced a 'Contract for Difference' (CFD) pilot scheme for 500 MW of renewable power. Managed by SECI, the scheme requires developers to supply 1,500 MW per hour during a three-hour non-solar period daily. CFD aims to stabilize revenues for green energy producers through a government-backed strike price, encouraging long-term investment.

articles.headline: Bank Credit Grows 15% as Total Loans Reach ₹209 Lakh Crore
articles.summary: According to RBI data, bank credit grew by 15% year-on-year to reach ₹209.21 lakh crore as of April 15, 2026. Deposit growth stood at 12.2%, totaling ₹256.48 lakh crore. While credit growth slowed slightly from the previous fortnight's 16.1%, the overall expansion reflects strong domestic demand. High credit activity at the end of the fiscal year also influenced the quarterly balance sheets.

articles.headline: Yellow Pea Imports Drop 50% Following New Import Duties
articles.summary: India's yellow pea imports fell sharply to 10.58 lakh tonnes between April and February, compared to 21.07 lakh tonnes in the previous year. The 50% decline is attributed to the imposition of import duties and high carryover stocks from domestic production. In February 2026, imports were just 57,434 tonnes, down from 1.46 lakh tonnes a year earlier as global prices outpaced domestic rates.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Forms Committee to Bridge Education-Employment Gap
articles.summary: NITI Aayog has established a high-level committee to address the disconnect between higher education and job market needs. The panel will identify high-priority service export sectors and assess the impact of AI on jobs. Chaired by the CEO of NITI Aayog, the committee includes members from the Labor, Skill Development, and IT ministries, along with industry bodies like NASSCOM and CII.

articles.headline: Pesticide Industry Demands Regulatory Data Protection in New Bill
articles.summary: CropLife India has urged the government to include a five-year Regulatory Data Protection (RDP) clause in the upcoming Pesticide Management Bill (PMB). The industry argues that without RDP, innovators cannot recover investments in new, safer molecules. Currently, pests and diseases cause 10-35% annual crop loss in India, valued at over ₹2 lakh crore, which new technologies could mitigate.

articles.headline: Cash Holding Rises as Gap Between Circulation and ATM Withdrawal Widens
articles.summary: An SBI report highlights a widening gap between Currency in Circulation (CIC) and ATM withdrawals, reaching ₹9,127 per person in FY26. This trend suggests a growing preference for holding precautionary cash despite the rise of UPI. CIC grew 11.9% to a record ₹41.6 lakh crore. The gap, which was ₹1,804 in FY24, correlates with rising income and periodic global economic uncertainties.

articles.headline: John Giannandrea to Succeed Tim Cook as Apple CEO
articles.summary: Apple has announced a major leadership transition where John Giannandrea, currently leading hardware and engineering, will become the CEO in September. Tim Cook will transition to the role of Executive Chairman. Giannandrea faces the immediate challenge of accelerating Apple's integration of Artificial Intelligence, where the company is perceived to be trailing rivals like Google and OpenAI.

articles.headline: PM Modi Highlights BJP's Women-Led Development Model in Bengal
articles.summary: At an election rally in Panihati, PM Narendra Modi criticized the TMC government for failing to protect women, citing the RG Kar hospital and Sandeshkhali incidents. He emphasized the BJP's 'women-led development' model and showcased the party's decision to field the mother of the RG Kar victim as a candidate. Modi also took a boat ride on the Ganges to highlight its clean waters.

articles.headline: Opposition Files Motion to Remove Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar
articles.summary: Opposition parties have submitted a notice to the Rajya Sabha Secretariat to move a resolution for the removal of Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar. The notice, signed by 73 MPs, lists nine specific allegations of misconduct and failure to uphold constitutional duties. Congress leader Jairam Ramesh stated that the CEC's actions have compromised the integrity of the electoral process.

articles.headline: IEA Warns of Global LNG Supply Deficit Due to US-Iran Conflict
articles.summary: The International Energy Agency (IEA) has lowered its global LNG supply growth forecast, citing significant damage to infrastructure in Qatar and the UAE due to the US-Iran conflict. An estimated 20 billion cubic meters of supply were lost in March-April alone. The agency warns that total losses could reach 120 billion cubic meters by 2030, potentially impacting 15% of expected global supply.

articles.headline: Iran Foreign Minister Araghchi in Pakistan to Discuss Peace Proposals
articles.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi visited Islamabad to consult with regional allies on a peace proposal regarding the conflict with the US. While no direct talks with American officials are scheduled, Pakistan and Oman are acting as intermediaries. Meanwhile, US President Donald Trump stated there is 'no rush' for a deal, emphasizing the need for a permanent solution to nuclear concerns.

articles.headline: Over 5 Lakh PNG Connections Provided Amid LPG Shortage
articles.summary: India has added 5.27 lakh Piped Natural Gas (PNG) connections since March to counter LPG shortages caused by the Middle East crisis. Government data shows 42,000 households surrendered LPG connections in favor of PNG. To assist migrant workers, oil marketing companies have sold 17.83 lakh small 5kg FTL cylinders. India relies on the Middle East for 90% of its LPG imports.

articles.headline: AI Hiring in India Grows 59.5% Led by Bengaluru Hub
articles.summary: A LinkedIn report reveals that AI-related hiring in India surged by 59.5% year-on-year. Bengaluru remains the global hub for AI talent, followed by significant growth in Hyderabad and Vijayawada. Companies across various sectors are integrating AI into daily operations, leading to high demand for engineers skilled in Large Language Models (LLM) and practical AI application development.

articles.headline: Karnataka approves 15% internal SC quota and 56,000 job recruitments
articles.summary: The Karnataka cabinet has approved internal reservation for Scheduled Castes, allocating 6% to the SC-Left, 5.25% to SC-Right, 4.5% to Touchables, and 0.25% to others. This move clears the way for the recruitment of 56,000 personnel into various government departments. The decision follows recommendations by the Justice A J Sadashiva Commission and a subsequent technical committee.

articles.headline: Assam court sentences 20 to life for 2018 mob lynching incident
articles.summary: A court in Assam has sentenced 20 people to life imprisonment for the 2018 lynching of two friends, Nilotpal Das and Abhijeet Nath, in Karbi Anglong. The victims were killed by a mob after being falsely accused of being child lifters. The case, which triggered national outrage, concluded with the conviction of the group under various sections of the IPC for murder and rioting.

articles.headline: Calcutta HC allows 12-hour bike ban during second phase of polls
articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court has modified the Election Commission’s 48-hour ban on two-wheelers, reducing the restriction to 12 hours on polling day (6 am to 6 pm). The court clarified that the ban should not apply to essential services, medical emergencies, or family functions. The ruling came in response to petitions challenging the total 48-hour prohibition originally imposed for the polls.

articles.headline: Netanyahu announces successful treatment for prostate cancer at age 76
articles.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu revealed that he has successfully undergone treatment for early-stage prostate cancer. The 76-year-old leader stated that the condition was caught in time and he is now healthy. The announcement comes amid ongoing regional tensions and domestic political challenges in Israel, providing clarity on his personal health status.

articles.headline: Pentagon explores military options to punish NATO allies over US access
articles.summary: A leaked internal Pentagon email suggests the US is considering suspending military operations with certain NATO allies that fail to support US missions or restrict access to their bases. Options include limiting joint exercises and intelligence sharing. The memo reflects growing frustration in Washington over the perceived 'reluctance' of some European partners to host US assets.

articles.headline: Trump rules out nuclear strike on Iran if elected president
articles.summary: Former US President Donald Trump stated he would 'never' use nuclear weapons against Iran, even in a conventional war scenario. He claimed that under his leadership, the US had decimated Iranian-backed groups and that nuclear weapons should never be used by anyone. The comments were made as he prepares for potential talks with international leaders via envoys Kushner and Witkoff.

articles.headline: Carlos Alcaraz withdraws from French Open due to forearm injury
articles.summary: World No. 2 Carlos Alcaraz has pulled out of the upcoming French Open after failing to recover from a persistent right forearm injury. The Spaniard, who also missed the Barcelona Open and Madrid Masters, stated he needs more time for rehabilitation. His absence from Roland Garros leaves a significant void in the men's draw, where he was considered one of the top favorites.

articles.headline: Sub-inspector accused of altering testimony in elopement case with 'not'
articles.summary: A sub-inspector in Shahjahanpur has been accused of maliciously altering a woman's testimony by inserting the word 'not' to falsely imply her elopement was without consent. The manipulation led to the arrest of the woman's lover. The officer has been suspended after the woman provided video evidence of her original statement and court testimony claiming the elopement was voluntary.

articles.headline: Karnataka HC rules dharma activities allowed in secular public auditoriums
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court has ruled that while India is a secular country, it does not mean dharmic and cultural activities cannot be performed in public spaces. The court set aside an order by a city corporation that had denied permission to a religious group to host a 'Shankaracharya Jayanti' event at an auditorium, stating that cultural heritage is an integral part of Indian life.

articles.headline: Rising summer temperatures cause Bengaluru city lakes to dry up
articles.summary: Several lakes in Bengaluru, including Channasandra and Kalkere, have dried up significantly as temperatures cross 35°C. Activists suggest this is a natural seasonal reset that provides an opportunity to desilt and remove plastic before the monsoon. Experts emphasized the need for a dedicated government department to maintain the city's 183 lakes and manage sewage inflow effectively.

articles.headline: Druggists raise alarm over counterfeit Mounjaro batches in Indian market
articles.summary: The Bangalore District Chemists' and Druggists' Association has flagged suspected counterfeit batches of the diabetes and weight-loss drug Mounjaro. Batches with 2025-2027 expiry dates were found to be suspicious in the Haryana and Food and Drug Administration (FDA) scans. Pharmacists have been instructed to halt sales of these specific batches to ensure public safety and patient health.

articles.headline: NIAS launches portal for mapping and documenting India's cultural heritage
articles.summary: The National Institute of Advanced Studies (NIAS) has launched KASTURI, a national digital platform for mapping India’s cultural heritage. The portal uses satellite imagery and GIS data to document monuments, temples, water systems, and historic routes into a unified framework. It aims to support researchers and conservation authorities in protecting vulnerable heritage sites.

articles.headline: Delayed white-topping project on Jayanagar 11th Main affects local businesses
articles.summary: A 10 crore white-topping project for a 500-meter stretch in Jayanagar has been delayed for six months, causing significant disruption to local commerce. Shop owners report a 50% drop in footfall due to blocked access and dust. Local MLA C K Ramamurthy stated that work on the remaining stretch and a major power line shift will begin within a month to resolve the long-standing issue.

articles.headline: Mario Miranda’s centenary celebration to feature special exhibition in Bengaluru
articles.summary: The 100th birth anniversary of legendary cartoonist Mario Miranda will be marked with a special exhibition at the Indian Cartoon Gallery from May 2 to May 23. Organized by the Indian Institute of Cartoonists, the event will showcase his iconic works depicting everyday life in Goa and Mumbai. Miranda was a Padma Vibhushan recipient known for his detailed and witty portrayal of people.

articles.headline: Lawyer seriously injured after lift malfunction at Bengaluru city club
articles.summary: A lawyer sustained serious fractures and dental injuries after a lift at a club in Kamakshipalya plummeted from the third floor to the ground. The victim and his friend were trapped for 15 minutes before the lift shook and fell. Police have registered a case of negligence against the club management for failing to maintain the elevator and lack of an emergency alarm system.

articles.headline: Bengaluru’s women bikers build community through weekend group rides
articles.summary: A growing number of women in Bengaluru are taking up motorcycle riding, forming communities like 'Nomadic Soul' and 'Riders and Heels on Wheels.' These groups organize training workshops and long-distance tours to empower women and challenge gender stereotypes. Members highlight that riding provides a sense of freedom and a supportive sisterhood for both beginners and veterans.

articles.headline: Karnataka seeks reduction in height curbs around Bengaluru airports
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has requested the Union Defence Ministry to reduce height restriction zones around HAL and Yelahanka airports from 20km to 15km. Deputy CM D K Shivakumar argued that the current restrictions are hindering vertical urban growth in the city's core areas. The state also offered land exchanges to facilitate expansion projects for the defence airports.

articles.headline: Infrastructure and waste issues dominate Bengaluru North corporation's public plaints
articles.summary: A grievance data analysis from the Bengaluru North City Corporation (BNCC) revealed that road maintenance and solid waste management are the top concerns for citizens, accounting for 38% of 1,626 complaints. While 73% of issues were reported as closed, residents expressed frustration over recurring problems like road-side garbage dumping, illegal parking, and poor footpath maintenance.

articles.headline: BengaluruOne centers to offer e-Swathu services for rural property regulation
articles.summary: Rural property owners in Karnataka will soon be able to access e-Swathu services at BangaloreOne and GramaOne centers across the state. The initiative aims to simplify the process of regularizing 97 lakh rural properties by providing digital records and khatas. The government will train operators at these centers to assist citizens who are not tech-savvy in navigating the online portal.

articles.headline: Panel suggests promoting wine and beer tourism to boost state revenue
articles.summary: A resource mobilization committee in Karnataka has recommended the promotion of wine and beer tourism to enhance state revenue. The proposal includes allowing vineyards to host tasting sessions and setting up wine boutiques in urban clusters. The move follows the successful model in Maharashtra and aims to integrate agriculture with the hospitality and tourism sectors to create jobs.

articles.headline: Karnataka government increases pension contributions for 14 state universities
articles.summary: In a relief for financially strained state universities, the Karnataka government has increased its contribution toward faculty and staff pension liabilities from 50% to 70%. Higher Education Minister MC Sudhakar stated that this move will improve the overall financial health of these institutions. The government has also partnered with various foundations for scholarship programs for girls.

articles.headline: Drones and satellite imagery expose illegal encroachments in Chhattisgarh tiger reserve
articles.summary: A mapping study by the Chhattisgarh forest department using Isro's satellite data and drones has revealed large-scale illegal encroachments in the Udanti-Sitanadi Tiger Reserve. Over 1,000 hectares of forest land were found to have been cleared and used for cultivation or settlements since 2008. Authorities have registered cases against the encroachers and are taking steps to restore the habitat.

articles.headline: First batch of 240 Bnei Menashe group immigrates to Israel
articles.summary: The first group of 240 members of the Bnei Menashe community from Mizoram and Manipur has landed in Tel Aviv as part of a mission to immigrate to Israel. The community, which claims to be one of the lost tribes of Israel, has been granted citizenship. More flights are planned to relocate the remaining 6,000 members of the community over the next few months to ensure their integration.

articles.headline: Aviation ministry pushes for hub-and-spoke model to streamline air travel
articles.summary: The civil aviation ministry is actively promoting a hub-and-spoke model to transform major Indian airports like Delhi and Bengaluru into international transit hubs. This model aims to consolidate domestic passengers at these hubs for seamless connection to long-haul international flights. The initiative is expected to enhance efficiency, reduce layover times, and boost the global competitiveness.

articles.headline: India secures LPG supplies through spot purchases amid West Asia crisis
articles.summary: To prevent domestic shortages, Indian oil marketing companies have secured additional LPG cargos through spot purchases from the Middle East and West Africa. The move comes as tensions in West Asia threaten traditional supply routes through the Strait of Hormuz. India imports nearly 60% of its LPG requirements, and the spot deals ensure a steady supply for households and commercial users.

articles.headline: Election Commission suspends police officer in West Bengal for alleged partisanship
articles.summary: The Election Commission has suspended a police officer in West Bengal’s North 24 Parganas for failing to act against political violence and showing bias. The decision followed reports of partisanship during the first phase of the assembly elections. The EC has directed that disciplinary proceedings be initiated and the officer be kept away from all poll-related duties in the future.

articles.headline: Norway to ban social media access for children under age 16
articles.summary: The Norwegian government has announced plans to introduce a bill that would ban children under the age of 16 from using social media platforms. PM Jonas Gahr Store stated the move is necessary to protect children from harmful algorithms and screen addiction. The law will require tech companies to implement strict age verification systems to ensure compliance and prioritize child safety.

articles.headline: Sensex slides 1,000 points as oil prices surge amid global tensions
articles.summary: The BSE Sensex crashed over 1,000 points on Friday, closing at 76,664 as rising crude oil prices and global uncertainty triggered a massive sell-off. Investors were spooked by the possibility of further escalation in West Asia, which could disrupt energy supplies and fuel inflation. The rupee also touched a new low against the dollar, further dampening market sentiment across various sectors.

articles.headline: Virat Kohli's 81-run knock leads RCB to victory over Gujarat Titans
articles.summary: In an IPL 2026 thriller, Royal Challengers Bengaluru defeated Gujarat Titans by five wickets, chasing down a target of 206. Virat Kohli was the star of the show with a brilliant 81 off 44 balls, including eight fours and four sixes. Sai Sudharsan's century for the Titans went in vain as RCB's middle order held their nerve to finish the game with seven balls to spare.

articles.headline: Assam scientist Atanu Nath among 2026 Breakthrough Prize winners
articles.summary: Dr. Atanu Nath, an assistant professor at Silchar’s Tihu College, has been named one of the recipients of the 2026 Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics. Often called the 'Oscars of Science,' the award recognizes his contribution to the Muon g-2 experiment at Fermilab. The experiment aims to explore the magnetic behavior of muons and potentially unlock new physics beyond the Standard Model.

articles.headline: India becomes top global destination for AI-related hiring in 2026
articles.summary: Data from the 2026 AI Labor Market Report shows that India has surpassed major economies to become the top global destination for AI-related hiring. Job postings in the sector jumped by 59.5% year-on-year, driven by a shift from pilot projects to real-world deployment. Bengaluru remains the primary hub, but there is significant growth in hiring across Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities as well.

articles.headline: AAP split as seven Rajya Sabha MPs join the BJP
articles.summary: The Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) faced a major crisis as seven of its ten Rajya Sabha members joined the Bhartiya Janata Party (BJP). The defectors include Raghav Chadda, Swati Maliwal, and Harbhajan Singh. Because the group represents more than two-thirds of the party's strength in the upper house, they are exempt from the anti-defection law. AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal called the move a betrayal.

articles.headline: Reliance Industries Q4 net profit dips 12.6% on energy business weakness
articles.summary: Reliance Industries reported a 12.6% decline in consolidated net profit to ₹18,951 crore for Q4 FY26, missing estimates. The energy (O2C) segment faced margin pressures due to global volatility and logistics disruptions. However, full-year profit rose 16% to ₹80,775 crore, supported by strong performances in the telecom and retail sectors. Annual revenue crossed ₹10.76 lakh crore.

articles.headline: RBI cancels Paytm Payments Bank license over persistent compliance failures
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has shuttered Paytm Payments Bank Ltd (PPBL) effective April 24, citing serious supervisory concerns and non-compliance with KYC norms. The central bank stated that allowing the bank to continue was detrimental to the public interest. The RBI will apply to the High Court for the winding up of the bank. One 97 Communications is transitioning services to other partners.

articles.headline: Govt Increases Wheat Procurement Target by 15% for Current Season
articles.summary: The central government has raised its wheat procurement target by 15% to 34.5 million tonnes to support farmers affected by unseasonal rains. The target was revised from an initial 30 million tonnes. Procurement expectations have been increased for states like Uttar Pradesh (25 lakh tonnes) and Rajasthan (23.5 lakh tonnes) as the government seeks to bolster national food security stocks.

articles.headline: Mercedes-Benz India Reports Strong Demand Despite Geopolitical Conflict
articles.summary: Mercedes-Benz India CEO Santosh Iyer stated that Middle East tensions have not impacted luxury car demand. The company has received over 400 bookings for its new electric models, which are sold out for several months. While supply chains face minor delays and increased shipping costs, vehicle production remains largely unaffected as demand from first-time luxury buyers grows.

articles.headline: Adani Green to invest ₹15,000 crore in battery storage
articles.summary: Adani Green Energy Ltd is earmarking ₹15,000 crore for battery energy storage systems (BESS) as part of a ₹45,000 crore FY27 capital expenditure plan. The company aims to commission 10 GWh of storage this year. While March quarter net profit rose 34% to ₹514 crore, annual profit dipped slightly due to exceptional items, including project write-offs in Sri Lanka and refinancing costs.

articles.headline: India's forex reserves jump $2.36 billion to hit $703 billion
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India reported that the country’s foreign exchange reserves increased by $2.362 billion to reach $703.308 billion during the week ended April 17. Foreign currency assets rose by $1.481 billion, while the value of gold reserves increased by $790 million. This follows a $3.8 billion increase in the previous week, indicating a strong external financial position.

articles.headline: Shriram Finance Q4 net profit soars 41% to ₹3,014 crore
articles.summary: Shriram Finance Ltd reported a 41% year-on-year jump in standalone net profit for Q4 FY26, reaching ₹3,014 crore. The NBFC's performance was driven by robust credit growth in commercial vehicles, passenger vehicles, and gold loans. Total assets under management grew by 15% to cross ₹3.02 lakh crore. The board has recommended a final dividend of ₹6 per share.

articles.headline: JP Morgan cuts Indian equities to neutral on AI surge
articles.summary: JP Morgan has downgraded Indian equities to 'neutral' from 'overweight', citing a stagflationary macro backdrop and the lack of AI exposure in large-cap indices. The bank raised its allocation for Taiwan and other tech-heavy markets. While valuations in India remain stretched at a 65% premium to emerging markets, JP Morgan cut its CY26 earnings growth forecast for India by 2%.

articles.headline: WMO warns of likely return of El Nino weather phenomenon
articles.summary: The World Meteorological Organization (WMO) has warned of a high probability that the El Nino weather phenomenon will return in the coming months. This could lead to a spike in global temperatures and altered rainfall patterns across the Indian subcontinent. The warming of ocean surfaces in the central and eastern Pacific typically triggers severe weather events, impacting agriculture and water.

articles.headline: Karnataka Cabinet Clears Internal Quota Within SC Reservation
articles.summary: Karnataka cabinet approved a revised sub-quota system for Scheduled Castes (SC) within the state's 15% reservation limit, allocating 5.25% each for right-hand and left-hand Dalits and 4.5% for other SC groups.

articles.headline: No Leaves for Medical Students Under This Year’s NEET Safeguards
articles.summary: Medical students are barred from taking leave on May 2-3 to prevent proxy candidates in NEET-UG 2026. The National Medical Commission advised colleges to deny leave except in exceptional circumstances.

articles.headline: Opposition submits fresh notice to remove Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar
articles.summary: A group of 73 Opposition MPs has submitted a fresh notice to the Rajya Sabha Secretariat seeking the removal of Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar. The motion cites 'proven misbehaviour' and partisan conduct, specifically pointing to social media posts and the handling of complaints during the current election cycle. This follows a previous notice that was rejected by the RS Chairman.

articles.headline: Calcutta HC rejects anticipatory bail for Congress leader Pawan Khera
articles.summary: The Gauhati High Court has denied anticipatory bail to Congress functionary Pawan Khera in a case involving alleged forgery and criminal conspiracy. The case was registered by the CM's wife, Riniki Bhuyan Sharma. The court observed that the custodial interrogation of the accused is necessary given the nature of the allegations involving the misuse of official letterheads.

articles.headline: 33K Indian Soldiers Commemorated on WWI Memorial
articles.summary: Names of 33,000 Indian soldiers who died in World War I, missing from an Iraq memorial, have been digitally commemorated. This marks a significant step in honouring their sacrifice.

articles.headline: ED raids 12 locations in Andhra liquor scam investigation
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted raids at 12 locations in Hyderabad and Tirupati in connection with a 3,200 crore liquor scam involving associates of former Andhra CM YS Jagan Mohan Reddy. The investigation focuses on alleged money laundering and the creation of shell companies to divert funds. The raids target individuals suspected of running a monopolized liquor cartel in the state.

articles.headline: Three killed and five wounded in fresh ethnic clashes in Manipur
articles.summary: Violence escalated in Manipur as three people, including a Tangkhul Naga and two Kuki-Zo individuals, were killed in separate gunfights between rival ethnic groups. Simultaneously, the NSCN (Aleng Group) claimed to have killed five members of Myanmar’s Kuki National Army. Security measures have been intensified in Ukhrul and Kamjong districts to prevent further escalation of hostilities.

articles.headline: Google to invest $40 billion in AI startup Anthropic
articles.summary: Google has announced a massive $40 billion investment in AI startup Anthropic as it ramps up efforts to compete with Microsoft and OpenAI. The funding will support Anthropic's development of advanced large language models and provide Google with exclusive access to its technology for cloud services. This marks one of the largest corporate investments in an AI firm to date.
